title: In re Yahoo! Inc. Customer Data Security Breach Litigation (2018) — MDL
technical_focus: Consumer harm theories in data breach actions

timeline.event: Public disclosures of large-scale account data incidents
District court opinion 313 F. Supp. 3d 1113 (motion to dismiss, standing, class issues)

primary_sources.label: District court opinion (313 F. Supp. 3d 1113) — CourtListener

evidence_artifacts: Forensic timelines and breach notification records
Identity monitoring and mitigation program documentation
engineering_takeaways: Breach litigation turns on harm theories and class treatment as much as intrusion mechanics.
Evidence of detection, scope, and consumer mitigation can matter for dispositive motions.
